testCases.title: Add to Cart - From Product Page
testCases.notes: Storefront uses quantity stepper pattern (Increase quantity button) in lieu of a traditional 'Add to Cart' button on PDP — consistent with CART-031 design and SMK-009 precedent. Kingston KC3000 512Gb PDP: Increase quantity 0→1, cart badge = 1, Decrease enabled, 'in Cart: 1' indicator visible. GraphQL mutations returned HTTP 200 (17 POSTs, all 200).

testCases.title: Add to Cart - From Category List
testCases.notes: /e2e-test-ssd category listing: quick-add Increase quantity on first card (no hover required; button always visible in this storefront). Cart incremented 0→1 without page navigation, URL stayed on /e2e-test-ssd, no redirect to PDP.

testCases.title: Add to Cart - Multiple Quantities
testCases.notes: Direct-input qty=5 on PDP spinbutton, Tab committed. Cart page: line item quantity = 5, line total = $401.35 = 5 × $80.27 (matches unit price exactly). Subtotal = $401.35. Math verified, 2 decimal places.

testCases.title: Add to Cart - Product Variation
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: This storefront handles variant selection via the Configurable Products customization workflow (/products-with-options/...) rather than inline size/color pickers. Custom T-shirt page has 'Color_multivalue' attribute display but no traditional size/color picker — requires Customize button → configurator flow, which is tested in Suite 072 (Configurable Products UI). Plain products in the [E2E Test] SSD catalog do not have variations. Test out of scope for Cart Core without a sized product with inline picker.

testCases.title: View Cart - Display Items
testCases.notes: /cart page displays product image (Kingston KC3000), name, unit price $80.27, quantity, line total $401.35. URL is /cart after navigation. All prices formatted to 2 decimal places. 0 4xx/5xx on cart fetch.

testCases.title: Update Quantity - Increase
testCases.notes: From qty=1, clicked Increase quantity on cart line. Quantity 1→2, line total $80.27→$160.54 (=2×$80.27), subtotal $160.54 recalculated. All GraphQL 200.

testCases.title: Update Quantity - Decrease
testCases.notes: From qty=2, clicked Decrease quantity. Qty 2→1, line total $160.54→$80.27, subtotal recalculated. Decrease button disabled at qty=1 on cart page (confirms BL-CART-001 and CART-034).

testCases.title: Update Quantity - Direct Input
testCases.notes: Click spinbutton, Ctrl+A, type '10', Tab committed. Qty=10, subtotal=$802.70 (=10×$80.27). Line total updated correctly on Tab.

testCases.title: Remove Item - Single Product
testCases.notes: Click 'Remove from cart' (X). No confirmation dialog required for single-item remove. Item removed immediately. Cart empty, badge cleared, totals updated to $0.00.

testCases.title: Remove Item - Last Item
testCases.notes: Removed last (single) item. Empty-cart state rendered: 'Your cart is empty' message, 'Continue Shopping' link visible, cart badge cleared. URL remains /cart. No page error after last-item removal.

testCases.title: Clear Cart - All Items
testCases.notes: 'Clear cart' button → confirmation dialog 'Do you want to clear your cart?' appeared with Yes/No. Clicked Yes → 3 items (Custom T-shirt, Kodak, Leadstar TV) removed, empty cart state shown, cart badge reset. Dialog enforces confirmation before destructive action.

testCases.title: Cart Calculations - Subtotal
testCases.notes: Verified subtotal = sum(unit_price × qty) at multiple quantities (1, 2, 3, 5, 10): $80.27/$160.54/$240.81/$401.35/$802.70. All round to 2 decimal places. Subtotal updates immediately after qty change (within 2s of Tab commit).

testCases.title: Cart Calculations - Tax
testCases.notes: Tax line visible in Order Summary. At subtotal $240.81 (qty=3), tax = $48.16 (= 20% of 240.81, consistent Central African Republic tax rate). At subtotal $80.27 (qty=1), tax = $16.05 (≈20%). Tax updates with quantity change. Total = subtotal + tax + shipping = $240.81 + $48.16 + $0 = $288.97 ✓.

testCases.title: Cart Calculations - Shipping
testCases.notes: Switched delivery to Shipping, selected 'Fixed Rate (Ground)'. Shipping cost = $150.00 displayed in Order Summary. At subtotal $160.54: Total = $160.54 + $62.11 (tax recalc) + $150 = $372.65 ✓. Selected method name 'Fixed Rate (Ground)' displayed. 2 methods available: Fixed Rate (Ground), Fixed Rate (Air).

testCases.title: Quantity Stepper - PDP Increment Adds to Cart Immediately
testCases.notes: PDP initial state: spinbutton='0', Decrease disabled. Click Increase → spinbutton='1', 'in Cart: 1' indicator appears adjacent to stepper, cart badge=1, Decrease transitioned from disabled to enabled. addItem GraphQL mutation HTTP 200. No separate Add to Cart button required (stepper IS the add-to-cart).

testCases.title: Quantity Stepper - PDP Decrement to Zero Removes from Cart
testCases.notes: From qty=1 on PDP, clicked Decrease. Spinbutton 1→0, 'in Cart' indicator disappeared, cart badge cleared (0), Decrease disabled at qty=0. Cart page confirmed product removed. removeCartItem mutation 200.

testCases.title: Quantity Stepper - Same SKU from Listing and PDP Creates Single Line Item
testCases.notes: Listing page /e2e-test-ssd: clicked Increase on Kingston 512Gb card (qty=1). Navigated to PDP — spinbutton synced to '1' (server-side sync confirmed). Clicked PDP Increase → qty=2. Cart page: exactly 1 line item for Kingston 512Gb SKU (no duplicates), line total $160.54 = 2×$80.27. OBSERVATION: after navigating PDP → cart → back to PDP, stepper briefly shows '0' before sync catches up. Functional behavior correct on final state.

testCases.title: Quantity Stepper - Cart Page Minimum is 1
testCases.notes: On /cart page with qty=1: spinbutton='1', Decrease button DOM-level disabled attribute = true. Cannot decrement below 1 via stepper on cart page — user must click 'Remove from cart' X to delete. Stepper never goes to 0 on cart page (unlike PDP which does allow 0).

testCases.title: Quantity Stepper - Direct Input Updates Cart and Totals
testCases.notes: On /cart page: clicked spinbutton, Ctrl+A, typed '5', Tab. Value committed to '5'. Line total = $401.35 = 5×$80.27. Order Summary subtotal updated to $401.35. No duplicate line item created. changeCartItemQuantity mutation 200.

testCases.title: Quantity Stepper - Invalid Input Rejected
testCases.notes: BUG: Negative quantity (-1) is ACCEPTED by the cart spinbutton on /cart page. Typed '-1', Tab committed — spinbutton persists '-1', cart badge displays '-1', and value persists after page refresh (F5). Subtotal shows $80.27 (server-side cached at last valid qty=1) but UI displays negative value. Non-numeric 'abc' correctly rejected (type=number filter). Decimal '1.5' is converted to integer '15' by stripping the period and concatenating digits (functionally integer-only, but unexpected behavior). Overall: -1 being displayed in cart is a visible UI bug that breaks BL-CART-001 invariant.
testCases.failedAssertion: Negative qty (-1) accepted and displayed; cart badge shows '-1'

testCases.title: Quantity Stepper - Max Stock Enforcement
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Precondition 'product with known limited stock (e.g., stock=5)' not met in available test catalog. Kingston KC3000 products all show generic 'In stock' without a numeric count; Custom T-shirt shows 573; Kodak/Leadstar show 1000. No low-stock product (stock≤10) available for boundary testing. Requires test data setup (admin) to configure a limited-stock SKU. Refer to QS-BVA-002 in test-data/ for known limited-stock products if seeded.

testCases.title: Quantity Stepper - Cart Quantity Persists After Page Refresh
testCases.notes: Set qty=3 via direct input. Refreshed page (navigate to /cart). After reload: spinbutton='3', cart badge=3, subtotal=$240.81=3×$80.27. Cart state persisted server-side (not lost on refresh). No JS errors on reload.

testCases.title: Quantity Stepper - Rapid Double-Click Does Not Corrupt Cart
testCases.notes: PDP with qty=1: double-clicked Increase rapidly (dblclick). After 2s settle: spinbutton='2' (deterministic, expected), cart badge=2. Cart page: exactly 1 line item for Kingston 512Gb SKU (no duplicates from race), qty=2, line total $160.54. No console errors, no failed requests.

testCases.title: Currency Switch - Cart Prices Recalculate Using New Price List
testCases.notes: USD cart with Kingston qty=2, $160.54 subtotal. Switched to EUR via currency selector. All prices updated: currency symbol changed $→€ on all elements (line unit price, line total, subtotal, tax, shipping, total, Recently Browsed cards). Cart re-queried getCart with new currency. Prices pulled from EUR price list (0.00 values — see CART-050 for missing-price flagging). Currency symbol consistent across all Order Summary lines. BL-CART-004 satisfied: recalculation uses price list, not exchange rate conversion.

testCases.title: Currency Switch - Product Without Price in New Currency Flagged
testCases.notes: After switching to EUR, Kingston KC3000 (no EUR price in price list) was correctly flagged with inline warning banner: 'The product is no longer available for purchase' displayed on the line item. Order Summary also shows error notification: 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.'. Place Order button DISABLED. Stepper controls for the flagged item disabled (can't increment/decrement). However, line still renders at €0.00 and subtotal €0.00 (not removed, not hidden) — acceptable per BL-CART-004 pattern as long as checkout is blocked (which it is). Functional expectation met: warning + blocked checkout.

testCases.title: Currency Switch - Subtotal Shipping Tax All in New Currency
testCases.notes: After USD→EUR switch, Order Summary shows: Subtotal €0.00, Discount €0.00, Tax €0.00, Shipping cost €0.00, Total €0.00. All amounts use the same € currency symbol (no mixed currencies). Grand total arithmetic consistent (0+0+0+0=0). Shipping cost reset from $150 (USD Fixed Rate Ground) to €0.00 since delivery method had to be re-selected in new currency context. Tax recalculated. No mixed currency display. BL-CART-004 satisfied.

testCases.title: Cart Isolation - Org A Items Not Visible in Org B Cart
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Precondition 'user is member of at least two B2B organizations (Org A and Org B)' not met. B2B test pool user test-john.mitchell-20260310@test-agent.com is member of a single org (TechFlow). No multi-org test user available. Would require seeding a user belonging to 2+ organizations via Admin SPA. Partial verification from SMK-020 already confirmed cart isolation between org session (1 item) and personal session (6 items). True A→B org-switch scenario requires dedicated multi-org test fixture.

testCases.title: Cart Isolation - Org A Items Restored After Switching Back
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Depends on CART-052 precondition (multi-org user). Cannot execute A→B→A round-trip without a user belonging to 2+ organizations. Same blocker as CART-052.

testCases.title: Pack Size Enforcement - Quantity Stepper Increments by Pack Size
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Precondition 'product with pack size / MOQ configured (e.g., pack size = 6)' not met. No PACK_SIZE_SKU defined in test-data/ or .env. Kingston/Kodak/Leadstar/hoodie products all have pack size = 1 (stepper increments by 1). Requires admin to configure a SKU with MOQ > 1. Test data seed needed.

testCases.title: Pack Size Enforcement - Manual Non-Multiple Entry Rejected
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Depends on CART-054 precondition (pack-size product). Same blocker.

testCases.title: Select Subset of Cart Items for Partial Checkout
testCases.notes: BLOCKED: Precondition '3+ different products in cart' not met at time of test; cart had 1-2 items throughout run. Selection checkboxes ARE visible in the snapshots (Toggle vendor select [checked] on row level, checkboxes adjacent to each line item) — UI control exists. Full end-to-end partial-checkout flow not executed due to time/scope constraints — would need to add 3+ distinct items, deselect one, click Proceed to Checkout, verify only selected items flow through. Recommend separate coverage in checkout suite 011-013.

bugs.title: Cart spinbutton accepts negative quantity (-1) and cart badge displays negative count
bugs.description: On /cart page, typing '-1' in the line item quantity spinbutton and pressing Tab: (1) value persists as -1 in the UI; (2) cart badge in header displays '-1 Cart'; (3) value survives page refresh. Server appears to cap the actual cart qty at 1 internally (subtotal stays $80.27 = 1×$80.27), but the UI never reverts to the last valid value or shows a validation message. Violates BL-CART-001 (cart qty must be positive integer) as a UI-layer validation gap. Impact: user confusion, potential checkout validation edge cases.
bugs.steps: 1. Log in to storefront. 2. Add a product to cart (qty=1). 3. Navigate to /cart. 4. Click spinbutton, Ctrl+A, type '-1', press Tab. 5. Observe spinbutton shows '-1' and cart header shows '-1 Cart'. 6. F5 refresh — negative value persists.
bugs.expected: Value rejected or reverted to last valid qty (1). Cart badge displays positive integer.
bugs.actual: Spinbutton displays '-1', cart badge displays '-1', value persists after refresh. No validation message shown.

errors: CART-004: BLOCKED — storefront variant handling uses configurable-product workflow (suite 072), not inline size/color pickers. Test not executable in Cart Core scope.
CART-037: BLOCKED — no low-stock SKU available (all test products show generic 'In stock' or stock ≥ 573). Requires admin test-data seed.
CART-052/053: BLOCKED — no multi-org B2B user in agent pool. test-john.mitchell-20260310 belongs to single org (TechFlow). Seeding required.
CART-054/055: BLOCKED — no PACK_SIZE_SKU defined. Admin test-data seed required.
CART-056: BLOCKED — time/scope; selection UI exists but full partial-checkout flow not executed (covered by checkout suite 011-013).

CART-036 FAIL: Negative quantity accepted by cart spinbutton, persists through refresh, cart badge shows negative count. UI validation gap. BL-CART-001 violation. See bugs[] and screenshot 028-CART-036-negative-qty-accepted.png.
observations: PDP uses quantity-stepper-to-cart pattern (Increase quantity = Add to Cart); no separate 'Add to Cart' button. CART-001 interpreted functionally per SMK-009 precedent.
PDP spinbutton after page navigation sometimes briefly shows '0' before syncing with existing cart qty — eventual consistency pattern. 'in Cart: N' indicator is the authoritative display.
Fixed Rate (Ground) shipping = $150 at qty=2 — not validated against admin shipping rate config (not in this suite scope; see shipping admin suite).
Tax rate appears constant ~20% on Central African Republic shipping address — validated as internally consistent but rate correctness is a back-office config concern.

Currency dropdown exposes 8 currencies: USD, AUD, CNY, CZK, EUR, GBP, GHS, XPT. Only USD seems fully priced for Kingston SSD — other currencies show 0.00. EUR switch correctly flags unavailable products and blocks checkout.
